paper_title: Skill Score Analysis of Precipitation and Temperature Assimilation

methods.description: This method evaluates the skill of precipitation, temperature, and sea-level pressure reconstructions by comparing correlation differences and RMSESS values between different assimilation experiments.

steps.description: Assimilate monthly precipitation amount data (exp_R) and compare the skill with the existing correlation between the CCC400 model simulation ensemble mean and the reference dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: compare skill of precipitation data assimilation with model simulation

steps.description: Analyze the correlation differences and RMSESS values for temperature and sea-level pressure across different months and regions.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze correlation and RMSESS for temperature and sea-level pressure

steps.description: Evaluate the effect of doubling the correlation length scale parameter on precipitation, temperature, and sea-level pressure reconstructions.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate impact of increased correlation length scale on reconstructions

steps.description: Conduct experiments with different observation types (temperature, sea-level pressure, precipitation amounts) and analyze the skill differences.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct and analyze experiments with different observation types

steps.description: Perform Shapiro-Wilk test for normality on the deviations of model ensemble members from the ensemble mean to assess the suitability of precipitation and wet days data for assimilation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Shapiro-Wilk test for normality on model deviations
